title: Luscious Japanese Rhubarb Soto Dashi
description: A refreshing and unique Japanese-inspired soup featuring spherified rhubarb in a clear broth. Perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Combine kombu, sake, and water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the kombu.
Add miso paste to the broth and whisk until dissolved.
Slice the rhubarb into 1-inch pieces and cook in the broth for 5 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix sodium alginate and citric acid with 2 tablespoons of water until dissolved.
Add the sodium alginate mixture to the rhubarb and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Use a spherification tool or a syringe to create small spheres of the rhubarb mixture.
Add the spheres to the broth and cook for 1 minute.
Season the broth with salt and stir in chopped green onion and sesame seeds.
Serve the soto dashi hot, garnished with additional green onion and sesame seeds if desired.
